meta.notes: DRAFT of the ELW Evening Prayer (Vespers) ordo. Structure verified against the authoritative Sundays & Seasons ordo (docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/12-sns-verification.md): Service of Light -> Psalmody (Ps 141) -> office hymn -> Reading + responsory -> Magnificat -> Prayers (litany / Lord's Prayer / collect) -> Blessing. The spurious threefold Kyrie has been removed (the S&S litany's embedded responses are not a standalone Kyrie); an office hymn added between Psalmody and the Reading; intercessions modeled as a prayers_of_intercession proper_slot. OPEN owner/taste calls from doc 12 not yet applied: Phos Hilaron is bundled here as the PD house light-canticle, whereas S&S's default in that slot is the [LIC] prose 'Thanksgiving for Light' (Phos Hilaron is one of eight optional Hymn-of-Light settings); blessing has no PD fallback (blank without entitlement); collect modeled day-variable vs. S&S's fixed alternatives. TEXT PROVENANCE per docs/research/2026-07-liturgy-strategy/10-pd-text-sourcing.md: bundles the traditional PUBLIC-DOMAIN default text (Robert Bridges' 1899 Phos Hilaron 'O gladsome light', 1662 BCP versicle/Gloria Patri, KJV Magnificat, ancient threefold Kyrie, traditional 'Our Father'). ELW's contemporary wording (ELW's Service-of-Light dialogue 'The light shines...', the ELW/ICEL Phos Hilaron 'O gracious Light', the ELLC Magnificat 'My soul proclaims', ELW's responsory, the Collect) is COPYRIGHTED and NOT bundled: left as [VAR]/[LIC] slots resolved via the church's Augsburg/S&S license. Element status: Service of Light dialogue=[LIC] (rubric, no PD default), Phos Hilaron=[PD Bridges 1899], opening versicle=[PD], Gloria Patri=[PD], psalmody=[VAR], reading=[VAR], responsory=[LIC] (rubric), Magnificat=[PD KJV], Kyrie=[PD ancient], Lord's Prayer=[PD traditional], collect=[VAR/LIC], blessing=[VAR/LIC].

blocks.note: Composite cover page from CongregationProfile. Identity/chrome, not liturgical text.

[LIC] The evening candle/lamp is lit. ELW's Service-of-Light dialogue ('Jesus Christ is the light of the world...' / 'The light shines in the darkness...') is copyrighted and resolves via the church's Augsburg/S&S license; only a neutral action rubric is bundled here.
blocks.text: The evening lamp or candle is lighted as the assembly gathers.

blocks.note: [PD] Phos Hilaron in Robert Bridges' 1899 English translation (public domain). The bundled traditional default; ELW's contemporary 'O gracious Light' (ICEL/ELW) is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: *GLORIA PATRI
blocks.note: [PD] traditional doxology, 1662 BCP form. In Lent the following 'Alleluia' is omitted (seasonal-customs layer).

blocks.title: Psalm 141
blocks.note: [VAR] The traditional evening psalm is Psalm 141 ('Let my prayer rise before you as incense'); the day's appointed psalm(s) may vary. Text/number from the day's appointment (S&S / lectionary).

blocks.title: *OFFICE HYMN
blocks.note: [VAR] Optional office hymn ('Song'), sung between the Psalmody and the Reading per the S&S ordo. render mode is per-document renderer-side.

blocks.title: READING
blocks.note: [VAR] Appointed lesson for the day. The response is a renderer-side inline literal.

blocks.title: RESPONSORY
blocks.note: [LIC] A responsory follows the reading. ELW's specific wording is copyrighted and resolves via entitlement; no PD default is authored here. Shown as a rubric so no ELW wording is bundled.
[PD] Magnificat, Luke 1:46-55, King James Version (public domain). The bundled traditional-language default; ELW's ELLC contemporary canticle is [LIC], resolved via entitlement.

blocks.title: INTERCESSIONS
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] The Evening Prayer litany ('In peace, let us pray to the Lord... Lord, have mercy', the sevenfold petition set per the S&S ordo), prayed before the Lord's Prayer. ELW's specific wording is copyrighted and resolves via the church's Augsburg/S&S license; no ELW litany text is bundled. Replaces the spurious threefold Kyrie the draft previously carried over from the Communion rite (the litany's embedded 'Lord, have mercy' responses are not a standalone Kyrie).

blocks.title: *LORD'S PRAYER
blocks.note: [PD] Reuses elw.lords_prayer, whose constant is the traditional 'Our Father, who art in heaven' wording (public domain).

blocks.title: COLLECT OF THE DAY
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] The appointed collect (S&S / ELW propers); resolves via entitlement. No PD fallback bundled.

blocks.title: *BLESSING
blocks.note: [VAR/LIC] Concluding blessing/benedicamus; resolves via entitlement. No PD fallback bundled.
